concepts.title: Zamindari System
concepts.description: The Zamindari system was a feudal system introduced by the British in India, where the Zamindar was responsible for collecting land revenue and paying it to the British government.

concepts.title: Peasant's Burden
concepts.description: The peasants' burden increased significantly during the British period due to the introduction of the Zamindari system and the heavy demand of land revenue, leading to widespread poverty and exploitation.

concepts.title: Revenue Collection
concepts.description: The British government used various methods to collect land revenue, including the Permanent Settlement of 1793 and the Bengal Board of Revenue, which led to increased exploitation of peasants.

concepts.title: Zamindari System Mnemonic
concepts.description: To remember the Zamindari system, use the mnemonic 'Z-R-E-P', which stands for Zamindar, Revenue, Estates, and Peasants.

concepts.title: Peasants' Condition
concepts.description: Students often assume that the peasants' condition improved during the British period due to the introduction of modern agriculture and infrastructure. However, this is incorrect as the peasants' burden increased significantly due to the Zamindari system and heavy land revenue.

concepts.title: Key Features of Zamindari System
concepts.description: Key features of the Zamindari system include the permanent settlement of land revenue, the role of the Zamindar as a collector of land revenue, and the exploitation of peasants for revenue collection.

concepts.title: Permanent Settlement of 1793
concepts.description: The Permanent Settlement of 1793 was a major policy introduced by the British government in India, which aimed to reduce the burden of land revenue on the Zamindars and make it more stable and predictable.

concepts.title: Peasant Movements
concepts.description: Peasant movements such as the Santal Hul and the Indigo Revolt emerged in response to the exploitation of peasants under the Zamindari system and the heavy demand of land revenue.
